name: Andvari
description: Andvari is a dwarf in Norse mythology known for his immense wealth and cursed ring, Andvaranaut. He lived beneath a waterfall and possessed the power to transform into a fish. His vast hoard of gold was coveted by many, including the gods. Loki, in particular, played a significant role in Andvari's downfall. Loki captured Andvari and forced him to surrender all his gold, including the ring Andvaranaut. Andvari, in his rage and despair, cursed the ring, declaring that it would bring misfortune and death to anyone who possessed it. This curse proved to be true, as the ring brought tragedy to several figures in Norse mythology, including the Volsung family and Sigurd. Andvari represents the dangers of greed and the destructive power of curses. His story serves as a cautionary tale about the corrupting influence of wealth and the consequences of betrayal. Though not a traditional hero, his story is central to several important narratives and his actions have far-reaching consequences in the Norse sagas. He is a complex character, driven by a desire to protect his wealth but ultimately undone by his own avarice and the cunning of others. He is a powerful sorcerer capable of shapeshifting, adding to his mystique and making him a formidable, albeit tragic, figure.
subtitle: Dwarf King of Cursed Gold
achievements: Accumulated a vast hoard of gold
Imposed a powerful curse on Andvaranaut

keyMyths.title: The Capture of Andvari
keyMyths.description: Loki captures Andvari in the form of a fish and forces him to surrender his gold, including the cursed ring Andvaranaut. This act sets in motion a chain of tragic events. The source is the Volsunga Saga and the Prose Edda.

keyMyths.title: The Curse of Andvaranaut
keyMyths.description: Andvari curses the ring Andvaranaut, declaring that it will bring misfortune and death to anyone who possesses it. This curse proves to be devastatingly effective. The source is the Volsunga Saga.

keyMyths.title: The Hoard and Fafnir
keyMyths.description: The gold hoard stolen from Andvari ultimately becomes the cause of Fafnir's transformation into a dragon and his subsequent death at the hands of Sigurd. The source is the Volsunga Saga.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A key source for Norse mythology, including the story of Loki's theft of Andvari's gold.
sources.text: A key source for Norse mythology, including the story of Loki's theft of Andvari's gold.

sources.author: Unknown
sources.description: A saga that details the story of the Volsung family and the curse of Andvaranaut.
